from a primary (to anticipate, usually with pleasure);

15.gl: a standing up again, i.e. (literally) a resurrection from death (individual, genitive case or by implication, (its author)), or (figuratively) a (moral) recovery (of spiritual truth)
15.kj: raised to life again, resurrection, rise from the dead, that should rise, rising again

24.gl: persuasion, i.e. credence; moral conviction (of religious truth, or the truthfulness of God or a religious teacher), especially reliance upon Christ for salvation; abstractly, constancy in such profession; by extension,

25.gl: decision (subjectively or objectively, for or against); by extension, a tribunal; by implication, justice (especially, divine law)
